trends.name: Hario Switch immersion drip hybrid brewing
trends.buzz: The Hario Switch continues to dominate pour-over forums as the beginner-friendly brewer that lets you toggle between immersion and percolation mid-brew. Reddit threads praise its forgiving nature and the way it turns mistakes into happy accidents.
trends.angle: Frame it as the training-wheels-optional brewer: you can start with full immersion for consistency, then experiment with hybrid recipes when you're ready. No wrong answers, just different cups.

trends.name: Single-dose grinder debate: DF64 Gen 2 vs. Fellow Opus
trends.buzz: r/espresso and r/pourover are debating whether the DF64 Gen 2's flat burrs justify the price jump over the Opus for pour-over purists. Both are praised for low retention and quiet operation.
trends.angle: Celebrate that both grinders exist at accessible price points. Emphasize that either choice levels up your game—pick based on budget and whether you want espresso flexibility later.

trends.name: Osmotic flow brewing (ultra-long steep experiments)
trends.buzz: Coffee nerds on r/JamesHoffmann are experimenting with 12–24 hour cold steeps using osmotic pressure principles, claiming smoother, tea-like clarity. Results are wildly inconsistent but the hype is real.
trends.angle: Position it as a fun weekend science project, not a daily method. Encourage curiosity: 'Try it once, compare notes, see if it's your vibe—no pressure to optimize.'

trends.name: Orea V4 flat-bottom brewer adoption spike
trends.buzz: The Orea V4's flat-bottom geometry and ribbed walls are winning over Kalita Wave fans who want more flow control. Baristas praise its even extraction and the option to use Kalita or custom filters.
trends.angle: Highlight it as a 'choose your own adventure' brewer—use the stock filters for speed or Kalita 185s for more body. Perfect for tinkerers who don't want to commit to one style.

trends.name: Anaerobic natural process beans flooding specialty roasters
trends.buzz: Roasters are releasing waves of anaerobic natural lots with fruit-bomb profiles (mango, passionfruit, wine-like). Social chatter suggests they're divisive—some love the funk, others find it too much.
trends.angle: Normalize the polarization: 'These beans are like natural wine—bold, funky, and totally fine to not be your thing. Grab a small bag and see where you land.' No judgment either way.

trends.name: Gooseneck kettle with pour-rate feedback (Acaia Orion)
trends.buzz: Whispers of a smart gooseneck kettle that tracks pour rate and weight in real time, helping beginners nail bloom timing and flow consistency. Specs and release date unconfirmed but the hype is building.
trends.angle: If real, frame it as optional training wheels for pour control—great for learning the feel, but you won't need it forever. Tech as a teaching tool, not a requirement.
